summary_en: Tender offer launched by Media Corp. (backed by Technoroji Inc.) to acquire Oricon Inc. at ¥1,331/share. Offer period: 23 May – 9 July 2024 (20 business days). Minimum acceptance threshold 50.10% of shares outstanding (excluding offeror's existing holdings). Purpose: take-private and delist. Board recommends acceptance; no stated premium over prior close disclosed in excerpt.
title_en: Notice Regarding Implementation of MBO and Recommendation of Tender Offer Participation
translation_en_brief: Oricon Inc. announced on 8 May 2024 that its Board has approved and recommends shareholder participation in a management buyout (MBO) tender offer. The offeror is Media Corp. (代表取締役: Masahiro Tomii), a special purpose vehicle established 17 March 2022 with capital of ¥300M, wholly owned by Technoroji Inc. The tender offer runs from 23 May to 9 July 2024 (20 business days) at ¥1,331 per share. The minimum acceptance threshold is 50.10% of shares outstanding (computed as 12,312,070 voting shares after excluding Oricon's treasury stock and JBCC-held trust shares, representing 1,231,270 votes). The offer's stated purpose is complete subsidiary status and eventual delisting of Oricon stock. The Board noted that implementation of this MBO contributes to long-term enterprise value and, in May 2023, decided to adopt a takeover defense framework (買収防衛策) with continuation approved at the 21st Annual Shareholders' Meeting in June 2024; no defensive procedures are being invoked in response to this offer. Oricon's Chairman Tsuneyo Koike, who holds 71,700 shares (0.58%), plans indirect reinvestment through family asset vehicle Litholland LLC, which holds 2,711,700 shares (22.11%).
